title: Fluffy Tunisian Almond Dumplings
description: Nutty, fluffy, kid‑friendly Tunisian dumplings infused with Thai green curry paste, cooked sous‑vide for a tender interior and finished in a quick sauté for a golden crust—low‑carb and easy to scale.
instructions: Whisk the eggs with coconut oil until creamy.
Stir in Thai green curry paste and lemon zest.
Add almond flour and salt; mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Gradually pour in cold water, a tablespoon at a time, until the dough is smooth, supple, and just cohesive.
Divide dough into 24 equal portions (~10 g each) and roll each into a small ball.
Place each ball into an individual vacuum‑sealed bag with a teaspoon of water to keep it from drying.
Vacuum seal the bags. Sous‑vide for 30 minutes. The dumplings will puff slightly as they cook.
Remove the bags, pat the dumplings dry with paper towels, then lightly sauté in a non‑stick pan with a splash of coconut oil until golden and airy—about 2 minutes per side.
Serve warm, garnished with chopped cilantro or a dollop of low‑fat Greek yogurt.
